observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Inventory Committee to tag all PPE items counted with property stickers with
complete vital information and ensure that tags/stickers are attached to prominent
and secure portions of the property to facilitate the easy identification of government
property;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the PPE Ledger Cards, for each
class of PPE;
c. Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and maintain Property
Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) upon issuance of PPE and ensure its renewal
every three years or every time there is a change in accountability or custodianships;
d. Inventory Committee to follow the procedures in the derecognition of non-
existing/missing PPEs under Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; for
the fair presentation of the PPEs in the financial statements; and
e. Municipal Accountant to provide proper disclosures on the non-existing/missing
PPEs, in accordance with Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006, to provide
additional information in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Assessor to assist the Municipal Engineer in the assessment of the fair
value of the road lot components of the Road Networks;
b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
General Services Officer the complete description and cost segregation of road
components;
c. Municipal General Services Officer to maintain the prescribed Local Road Network
Property Card for all roads and its components; and
d. Municipal Accountant to:
• maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for all roads and its components
and recompute the depreciation per road components, except for the road lot
in accordance with the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2015-008 and make the
necessary adjustments in the books of account; and
• disclose the complete information of the Road Network System in accordance
with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and
Municipal Accountant to use the OMOE account only for expenditures that cannot be
classified under specific expense accounts in accordance with IPSAS 1, COA Circular
No. 2015-009 and Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust
the accounts by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the misclassified
accounts, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence
of the unliquidated fund transfers and prepare aging schedule on a quarterly basis
(Annex 3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005); and
b. submit to the Audit Team the request for the write-off of dormant unliquidated
fund transfers, supported by the necessary documents enumerated in item 8.3 of
COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and issue the ICS for semi-
expendable property totaling P194,608.00, and to likewise prepare and maintain
the SPC, RegSPI, and RSPI, for the establishment of proper control and
accountability over the semi-expendable property; and
b. Municipal Accountant to maintain the SPLC to record the acquisition, description,
custody, impairment, issue/transfer/disposal, repair history, and other information
about the property.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer to prepare the corresponding IIRUP by filling-
out the inventory portion and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor and to
the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to initiate the immediate disposal of the
unserviceable properties, in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and the
rules and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 92-386;
b. Appraisal and Disposal Committee to conduct an appraisal of the unserviceable
properties and submit the appraisal report to the Office of the Auditor; and
c. facilitate the disposal of the Unserviceable Properties promptly through sale or
negotiation to prevent further deterioration and loss of additional income.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services
Officer to:
a. provide the lacking information to the noted 348 POs and adhere with Item B of
COA Circular No. 96-010 on the preparation of PO, complete with all the necessary
information pertaining to the procurement to ensure that the supplier will deliver the
items in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract; and
b. furnish the Office of the Auditor of AIRs to notify the time and date of every
scheduled delivery of procured items within 24 hours from its acceptance, in
compliance with Item A(2) of COA Circular No. 96-010.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to require all implementing units to prepare and submit
Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) for all of their different programs,
activities, and projects, evaluate the PPMPs as to consistency with the
Municipality’s budget, and submit the same to the BAC Secretariat for
consolidation in accordance with Sections 7.3.2 and 7.3.4 of the Revised IRR of
RA No. 9184;
b. BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMPs into a complete APP, ensure that all
relevant information is indicated in accordance with the prescribed format in Item
4.1 GPPB Circular No. 07-2015, and submit the plans to the BAC for final
recommendation; and
c. the Municipal Budget Officer, BAC, and BAC Secretariat to regularly monitor and
update the APP when the need arises.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Local Development Council,
through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and in
coordination with Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. ensure that the planned PPAs under the 20 percent DF are well-planned and
procurement-and- implementation-ready; and
b. identify the specific PPAs to be funded from the 20 percent DF and refrain from
the inclusion of lump-sum or generic terms to ensure the optimal utilization of the
fund.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMC
through the MDRRMO to include and present under a separate caption the list of
specific PPAs to be funded from the previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF, indicating
therein the specific funding source of the year of transfer of each PPA in the
LDRRMFIP, in accordance with item in 5.1.2 and Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-
002.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal General Services Officer has already prepared the PCs while the Municipal Accountant is currently in the process of maintaining PPELCs.

The Municipal Engineer explained that some data are still incomplete due to lack of available records especially for projects before her assumption as Municipal Engineer but she assured the completion of the needed data within the available means.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate financial statements and ensure fair presentation of accounts for the 20 percent Development Fund, in compliance with Section 313 (c) of RA No. 7160 and Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already prepared the separate financial statements for the 20 percent development fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers of unexpended LDRRMF by year of transfer and based on different sources; and
b. Municipal Accountant to fully disclose the details of the previous years’ unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements, as required under Item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already maintained separate subsidiary ledgers of the transferred unexpended LDRRMF based on different sources. Details on the previous years’ unexpended balances of the LDRRMF are already disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements, as required under Item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare Schedule of Unreleased Checks monthly based on the actual release or issuance to the payees and forward the same to the Municipal Accountant, in compliance with Section 2.3 of GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) at year-end to record the restoration of unreleased checks and recognize the appropriate liability accounts to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements, in compliance with Section 2.4 of GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001, and enter the gross amount in the Registry of Accounts Written Off upon authority (if applicable).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already prepared the schedule of Unreleased Checks monthly based on the actual release or issuance to the payees and forwarded to the Municipal Accountant for recording. Before the JEV was drawn, the checks are already released to the payees. For CY 2024, released checks were not included in the list of outstanding checks reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the unliquidated fund transfers and prepare aging schedule on a quarterly basis (Annex 3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005); and
b. submit to the Audit Team the request for the write-off of dormant unliquidated fund transfers, supported by the necessary documents enumerated in item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is currently conducting the investigation of the dormant unliquidated fund transfers. According to her, she has already reached out to the former Department Heads but no response has been received. She will facilitate the preparation of the request for write-off of dormant unliquidated fund transfers upon completion of required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer to prepare the corresponding IIRUP by filling-out the inventory portion and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor and to the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and the rules and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 92-386;
b. Appraisal and Disposal Committee to conduct an appraisal of the unserviceable properties and submit the appraisal report to the Office of the Auditor; and

observations.managementAction: The Municipal General Services Officer expressed her commitment, together with the Appraisal and Disposal Committee, to prioritize the disposition of the unserviceable properties.

Provide the lacking information to the noted 96 POs and adhere with Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 on the preparation of PO, complete with all the necessary information pertaining to the procurement to ensure that the supplier will deliver the items in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract; and
b. Furnish the Office of the Auditor of AIRs to notify the time and date of every scheduled delivery of procured items within 24 hours from its acceptance, in compliance with Item A(2) of COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: The details on the noted 96 POs are already provided by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC), through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to:
a. Present under a separate caption the list of specific PPAs to be funded from the previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF, indicating therein the specific funding source of the year of transfer of each PPA in the LDRRMFIP, in accordance with item in 5.1.2 and Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
b. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records for them to properly monitor and fully disclose the details of the unexpended balances by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO will include the PPAs under Special Trust Fund, including the specific funding source in the next planning period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. LDRRMC through the MDRRMO to ensure submission of the revised LDRRMF AIP to the Local Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator for deliberation and inclusion in the AIP; and
b. Municipal Accountant to check if the PPA is included in the LDRRMFIP and before processing the release and utilization of the funds ensure proper inclusion in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: All of the available appropriations are already included in the LDRRMF AIP. The Municipal Accountant has reviewed the inclusion of PPAs in the AIP before processing the payments to the suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the granting of cash advances to AO in excess of his maximum cash accountability, otherwise increase the fidelity bond of AO sufficient to cover his accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The bond for AOs with exceeding maximum cash accountability has been increased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer to identify specific PPAs to be funded from the 20 Percent Development Fund and refrain from the inclusion of lump-sum or generic term PPAs in the AIP to ensure optimal utilization of the appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Projects that are not implemented in CY 2024 encountered problems in design and implementation schedules, but she ensured that these projects will be implemented in CY 2025. For PPAs that are stated in generic terms, he is committed to revise the Annual Investment Plan for CY 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer to:
a. Formulate specific written policy guidelines for the engagement of JO workers that will define the conditions for the hiring and will set the criteria for fair placement and justified daily wage rates; and
b. Review the current organizational structure and consider to fill vacant positions or to create additional permanent positions, when necessary, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and in accordance with the existing budgeting and accounting rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Specific guidelines are already formulated by the Human Resources Management Officer. The current organizational structure has been reviewed by the Human Resources Management Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to establish a complete GAD Database containing gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data and to establish a complete GAD Database containing gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data to identify gender issues based on the gender analysis, in accordance with Section 36 (c) of RA No. 9710 and Item 4.1 B of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person has coordinated with the Philippine Statistics Authority to furnish them the latest gender statistics, and has already made a follow-up, however, they are still waiting for the latest data which will be used in gender analysis.

b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer the complete description and cost segregation of road components;

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate financial statements and ensure fair presentation of accounts for the 20 percent Development Fund, in compliance with Section 313 (c) of RA No. 7160 and Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken on the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant followed the previous years’ presentation of the financial statements due to the limited time of preparation, only the consolidated financial statements for the General Fund were prepared. A separate set of financial statements for special account such as the 20 percent Development Fund is necessary to establish its actual financial status and performance. Hence, the inability to prepare a separate set of financial statements deprived the Municipality and other users of vital information relative to the assets, liabilities, equity and financial performance of the fund. We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate financial statements and ensure fair presentation of accounts for the 20 percent Development Fund, in compliance with Section 313 (c) of RA No. 7160 and Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Exert more effort to secure copy of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and other relevant data of the fund transfers, review the terms and conditions and if stipulated return the unutilized balances to the respective Source Agencies, in accordance with Item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;
b. Submit to the Audit Team the request for the write-off of dormant unliquidated fund transfers, supported by the necessary documents enumerated in COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
c. Conduct regular and periodic verification, validation and reconciliation to the Source Agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant exerted efforts to secure copy of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and other relevant documents of the noted fund transfers, review the terms and conditions and return the unutilized balances to the respective Source Agencies. However, she was unable to retrieve any pertinent documents. Nonetheless, all new fund transfers from NGAs were supported with MOA and other relevant documents. The Municipal Accountant plans to request for the write-off of dormant unliquidated fund transfers in 2024, coinciding with the dormancy of the GMA Financial Assistance balance in 2023.
observations.reason: No action was taken on the audit recommendation. The Municipal Accountant attempted to retrieve MOAs and relevant documents but none were found; she plans to file request for write-off of dormant unliquidated fund transfers in 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant:
• In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to correct the erroneous Journal Entry Voucher transfer of P500,000.00 that should have been retained in the General Fund as a Continuing Appropriation and revert P46,460.67 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund; and
• Ensure proper accounting and recording of the unexpended balances in compliance with Items 5.1.10, 5.1.11 and 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to request to the Members of Sangguniang Bayan an enactment of ordinance for the availability of P46,460.67 for other expenditures and categorized as appropriations for other expenditures; and
c. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to prioritize the utilization of prior years’ unexpended balances that is about to lapse in the five-year period.
observations.managementAction:
a. Municipal Accountant:
• In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, corrected the erroneous Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 100-2023-05-059 and 300-2023-05-011, both dated May 24, 2023, to correct the erroneous transfer and to revert the unappropriated surplus to the General Fund, respectively.
• Ensured proper accounting and recording of the unexpended balances as provided in Item 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
b. Municipal Budget Officer:
• Requested the enactment of Ordinance No. 01 to allocate the available fund of P46,460.67 for other expenditures. On April 28, 2023, Ordinance No. 01 was passed to allocate the said fund.
c. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer:
• Prioritized the utilization of the unexpended balances by formulating Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) to maximize the use of the fund.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the necessary JEVs to correct the erroneous transfer and to revert the unappropriated surplus to the General Fund; Ordinance No. 01 was enacted to allocate the available balance of P46,460.67 for other expenditures; and the MDRRMO prioritized the utilization of the unexpended balances by formulating PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to examine the facts and extent of the delays of the contractor and recommend the approval or disapproval of the Contract Time Extension, if warranted and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to account for the negative slippage and impose liquidated damages;
b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat, if approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity, to submit the Contract Time Extension, PERT/CPM and revised Construction Schedules, and Project Evaluation Review Technique/Critical Path Method (PERT/CPM) to support its revised target completion date; and
c. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to closely monitor the performance of the contractors for all of the ongoing projects and recommend measures in case of non-adherence to the contract.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer and other concerned Municipal Officials to:
a. Submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies;
b. Send a written communication to the Contractor to require the submission of the lacking documents mentioned and to provide a clear computation of the number of days needed for the contract time extension; and
c. Recommend only the approval of the contract time extension if is supported with time complete supporting documents, in compliance with the provisions of R-IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Based on the examination conducted by the Municipal Engineer, the approval of the Contract Time Extension was justifiable. The management requested the contractor to submit the lacking documents. The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator closely monitored the contractors' performance for all ongoing projects and made recommendations as necessary. The management requested the contractor to submit the revised Construction Schedules, and PERT/CPM. The Audit Team received a memorandum dated May 8, 2024 from the COA Regional Office No. IV-A on May 20, 2024 regarding the results of the technical evaluation conducted on the said project, requesting submission of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Some deficiencies were overlooked initially but the Engineer asserted the approval was justifiable and requested missing documents; outstanding documents were requested from the contractor and COA followed up via memorandum; some required submissions are still pending from the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer to:
a. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to correct the
misstated accounts; and
• stop the practice of deducting the 15 per cent mobilization fee/advance
payment on the first progress payment, instead systematically recoup the 15
per cent mobilization fee/advance payment over the construction period
based on the periodic SWA, in compliance with Items 4.3 and 5.3 of Annex E
of R-IRR of RA No. 9184.
b. Municipal Engineer to ensure the percentage of completion and amount to be
certified as progress payment is equal to the actual accomplishment at a certain
date.
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer agreed to comply with the audit
recommendations. The Municipal Accountant also committed to effect the necessary
adjustments in the books to record the unrecorded accomplishments of infrastructure
projects and to correct the erroneous entries made in the recoupment of the mobilization
fees/advance payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. record the noted Road Networks individually per project with complete details
in the Local Road Network Ledger Card;
b. trace the construction completion date, recompute the depreciation per project
and make necessary adjustment in the books of account; and
c. ensure that infrastructure assets are recorded and depreciated individually per
projects, in accordance with IPSAS 17.
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations and
committed to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to record the noted Road
Networks and provide correct depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that every transactions are recorded in the books using the correct
accounts, in compliance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs as prescribed
by COA Circular No. 2015-009.
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendation and
committed to observe the recording of transactions using the proper accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

forward the same to the Municipal Accountant, in compliance with Section 2.3
of GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001; and

the restoration of unreleased checks and recognize the appropriate liability
accounts to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial
statements, in compliance with Section 2.4 of GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-
001.
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant both agreed to comply with the audit
recommendations. The Municipal Accountant committed to ensure that adjustments
related to unreleased checks will be made at year-end based on the schedule submitted
by the Municipal Treasurer.

both by P436,696.70; and

or production of a qualifying asset should be capitalized as part of the cost of
the asset, in accordance with the pertinent provisions of IPSAS 5.
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations and
committed to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to capitalize the noted
borrowing cost.

The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations and
committed to follow the aforementioned rules and regulations on the recording of

prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for
In-kind Donations, Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations,
Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods and

Equipment (PPE) and furnish the same to the National Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Council (NDRRMC), through the Office of the Civil Defense
(OCD), copy furnished the Audit Team, within ten working days after the end of
each quarter, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. draw Journal Entry Vouchers to reclassify the PPE items totaling P733,286.98 to its appropriate account; and
b. drop the semi-expendable property by debiting the Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity accounts by P1,827,411.35 and P411,433.85, respectively, and crediting the respective PPE accounts by P2,238,845.20.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has drawn Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 2020-100-08-198 dated 8/31/2020 to reclassify the aforementioned PPE items. JEV No. 2020-100-08-197 dated 8/31/2020 was drawn to drop the semi-expendable property.

The Inventory Committee conducted the physical count of the road networks. However, the same were not completed as year-end. No LRNPC and LRNPC were prepared. Subsidiary Ledgers were prepared and maintained by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The physical count was not completed due to lack of time and available personnel. Incomplete records were maintained and turnover between the former and current Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to address the difference in the accounts Due to/from LGUs and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that the balances of the accounts are in agreement. Moreover, regularly furnish the Provincial Accountant with the copy of Journal Entry Voucher and/or summary of collections, as basis in recording in the books of the Municipality and Province, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant to address the aforementioned differences. The subsidiary ledgers were prepared and maintained by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: There remained unreconciled items between the Municipality and the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to correctly classify the outstanding balance of the long-term loans payable by presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within twelve months in the amount of P2,125,252.52 as part of the current liabilities and the remaining balance of P8,563,636.38 as non-current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant correctly classified the current and non-current portion of the loans payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required supporting information in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS.
observations.managementAction: Required information were already disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS. The inventory committee provided inputs for Road Networks which were not completed.
observations.reason: Complete information for the Road Networks account was not yet available since the physical count had not yet been completed by the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. strictly comply with the accounting guidelines enumerated in the Revised Chart of Accounts under COA Circular No. 2015-009 for the fair presentation of financial statements; and
b. correct the erroneous accounting entries in the General Fund (GF) books and transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund (TF) books by debiting Trust Liabilities – DRRMF and crediting Due to Other Funds in the books of General Fund and debiting Due from Other Funds and crediting Trust Liabilities – DRRMF in the books of Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the Revised Chart of Accounts under COA Circular No. 2015-009 and corrected the Journal Entry Voucher No. 2019-100-12-1773 dated December 27, 2019 to adjust the transfer of the unexpended balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the loans and insurance applications of the employees and ensure that payroll deductions are in accordance with the order of preference provided by RA No. 11260 and that the employees' net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum amount of P5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: New applications for loans were strictly assessed and monitored by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to properly identify and categorize programs and projects that are to be charged to LDRRMF in accordance with Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; b. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF; and c. instruct the LDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the COA Auditor as required under 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 for monitoring and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF were prepared and submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal Management aligned utilization to projects allowed under the guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials to:
a. coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to establish a centralized GAD database to have a source of reliable information necessary for its GAD planning, programming and policy formulations. Meanwhile, utilize the existing database, planning and data-gathering tools and management information system to generate gender and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources, such as surveys and studies; and
b. include in the GAD Accomplishment Report the documents enumerated in Section 4.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to facilitate the evaluation of reported accomplishments.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already taken initial measures in the process of establishing the GAD Database. Meanwhile, utilize existing data-gathering tools. The GAD Accomplishment Report with supporting documents were submitted.
observations.reason: The Management was still in the process of establishing the GAD Database and completing required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials to:
a. effectively plan the priority development programs, projects and activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) by considering its feasibility and necessity; and
b. maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent development budget to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management exerted efforts to prioritize and implement projects under the 20 per cent Development Fund. Several projects were implemented but full utilization was affected by the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Implementation of several projects under the 20 per cent Development Fund was delayed due to the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the MSWMB to review and update the Ten-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan every two years thereafter or as the need arises in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9003; and
b. establish a Solid Waste Management program and revise the composition of the MSWMB to conform with Section 5, Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 to optimize the involvement of all sectors as envisioned by the law.
observations.managementAction: The Ten-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan was reviewed by the MSWMB. Executive Order No. 008-S-2021 was issued to revise the creation of the MSWMB.
observations.reason: The revised MSWMB did not yet fully conform with the membership requirement of the IRR of RA No. 9003 (e.g., still did not include the Chairperson of the Sangguniang Kabataan Federation and a representative from the manufacturing or packaging industry).

and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to:

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to:
• fast tract the reconciliation of the PPE account balances per books and per RPCPPE, and make the necessary adjustments; and
• maintain and update PPE Ledgers Cards and Property Cards, respectively, provide complete and necessary information in both records to facilitate verification and reconciliation and to effect the necessary adjustments in their records.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer was able to reconcile their records in CY 2019.
Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards were updated by the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Treasurer were still gathering information on some PPE items that were purchased several years ago.
observations.reason: The Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards were updated by the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Treasurer were still gathering information on some PPE items that were purchased several years ago.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for purchases/ requisitions of supplies in compliance with Section 51 of MNGAS for LGUs;
b. record the inventory issuances based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) prepared and submitted by the General Services Officer/ Supply Officer; and
c. maintain the Ledger Cards for supplies by type of inventory as of a given date; and
• General Services Officer/ Supply Officer to:
a. prepare and submit monthly the RSMI and the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant for the respective recording of issuances and for reporting the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date; and
b. maintain Stock cards for supplies for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant was able to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in CY 2019.
SSMI was not prepared by the Municipal General Services Officer. SSMI was not prepared. Stock cards for supplies were prepared by the Municipal Accountant. Physical count of supplies were conducted and corresponding RPCI was already submitted by the concerned Municipal Officials.
observations.reason: SSMI was not prepared by the Municipal General Services Officer. Stock cards prepared by the Municipal Accountant were not in accordance with the format prescribed by MNGAS for LGUs. Physical count reports (RSMI) were still not prepared by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate submission of the 14 DVs and their supporting documents. Henceforth, require the submission of accounts within the period prescribed under Section 100 of PD No. 1445 and otherwise avail of the remedies provided under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 in instances of unjustified failure to submit said accounts.
observations.managementAction: DVs were already submitted by the Municipal Accountant. Henceforth, require the submission of accounts within the period prescribed under Section 100 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006, otherwise avail of the remedies provided under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 in instances of unjustified failure to submit said accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s required that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances totaling the audit P244,600.00 in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 97-002. Henceforth, see to it that all outstanding cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Management required that cash advances be liquidated within the prescribed period. The audit recommended liquidation of cash advances confirmed the compliance of the Municipality’s officers and employees with Section 5.8 of COA Circular Nos. 97-002.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to take immediate action to transfer the titles of the acquired land in the name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the Municipal Government. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund for the transfer of titles of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management already initiated several steps in transferring the titles of the acquired land under the name of the Municipality. Funds were appropriated by the Municipal Budget for the aforementioned purposes.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still completing the other documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services to:
• expedite the registration and/ or renewal of registration of the seven Motor Vehicles with the Land Transportation Office to avoid impounding, payment of fines and penalties and non-indemnification in case of accidents or loss; and
• facilitate the transfer of titles of motor vehicles with the donors of the subject vehicles for the transfer of the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership and control over the vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The Management already coordinated with the LTO to facilitate the registration and renewal of the registration of the seven Motor Vehicles. The Management also coordinated with the donors to facilitate the transfer of titles of the vehicles.
observations.reason: Some documents required by the LTO for the registration and renewal of the Motor Vehicles are still being retrieved by the Management. The Management are still awaiting for the replies of the donors.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately undertake an appraisal of these unserviceable motor vehicles and prepare a corresponding report thereon;
• direct the MGSO to submit to COA Auditor a request for disposal of all unserviceable property together with Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property, Appraisal Report and other required documents for his/ her appropriate action; and
• direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop from the books of accounts the property subject of disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Appraisal and Disposal Committee was not able to appraise the unserviceable motor vehicles due to the voluminous work load. Upon completion, request for disposal of the unserviceable property will be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for inspection and determination of PPE’s value.
observations.reason: Appraisal of the unserviceable motor vehicles was not conducted, similarly, IIRUP was not submitted. Disposal of Unserviceable property were not yet undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Designated Municipal General Services Officer see to it that all property of the Municipality are insured with the General Insurance Fund of GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to insure some of the property of the Municipality with the GSIS. The Management prioritized those property that are more valuable and prone to damage or loss.
observations.reason: Funds for CY 2019 is not enough to cover the insurance of all property of the Municipality, the ones that are more valuable and prone to damage or loss were prioritized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit to the National Treasury or the Source Agencies some of the unexpended balances of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to remit to the source agencies some of the unexpended balances of the fund. The Management already initiated steps to facilitate the return of the unexpended balances.
observations.reason: Unexpended balances which were outstanding from four months to more than three months are still being coordinated with the source agencies for the return of the unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Secretariat of the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee to submit copies of Contracts/POs and its supporting documents to the concerned Auditor within five working days from their execution/ issuance; and that the Municipal General Services Officer to furnish copies of delivery documents to the concerned Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of delivery of goods and services.
observations.managementAction: The POs were submitted regularly by the Management. Compilation of the Auditor supporting the submission of the contracts is delayed.
observations.reason: Contracts for several projects of the Municipality were still not submitted within five working days from its execution. Notice of deliveries were still not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC officials to post Notices and approved contracts in aforesaid PHILGEPS website, in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Notices of Award and Proceed for CY 2019 were posted in the PHILGEPS website of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert the unexpended balance of CY 2013 LDRRMF appropriation back to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund for subsequent re-appropriation by the Local Sanggunian for other social services, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to revert LDRRMF balances back to the Unappropriated Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator to:
• ensure that prioritized projects under the 20 per cent development fund are fully implemented as planned; and
• maximize the use of the 20 per cent Local Development Fund to ensure its full implementation.
